summary: A good audience recording captures the magical Oxford ’88 show, highlighted by a blistering Hey Pockey Way and an rousing Mr. Fantasy/Hey Jude suite despite a cheesy Little Girl Lost.
review: This audience tape is considered the best available for Oxford 7‑3‑88 – clear enough to enjoy but not pristine – and it documents one of the most peaceful, groovy Dead scenes many fans recall. The performance shines with strong first‑set staples like Sugaree and Birdsong, a standout second‑set opener (Hey Pockey Way), and an electrifying Mr. Fantasy/Hey Jude medley, though the Little Girl Lost segment falls flat.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews praise the musical performance strongly (70% weight) while noting the recording is good but not stellar (30% weight), leading to an overall solid rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as the best audience source for this date – satisfying and clear enough, though not a flawless or “stellar” recording.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong first set with Sugaree and Birdsong; second set launches with a blistering Hey Pockey Way but is cut short, then recovers with a lively post‑drums sequence and an outstanding Mr. Fantasy/Hey Jude medley.

band_member_comments.Keys: Brent's playing is highlighted as both a strength and a weakness, bringing out the best and worst of this very good show.
